rulings.Case Title: RANDALL AYRES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 5, 2007, LINE 1 DEFENDANT SLAKEY BROTHERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELEGANT ILLUSIONS, INC. VS. JMA VENTURES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-06-619980, CUD-07-621016 WITH CASE CGC-06-454195
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 5, 2007, LINE 2 DEFENDANT JMA VENTURES, LLC's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-06-619980, CUD-07-621016 WITH CASE CGC-06-454195. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO CASE #454195 AND CASE #621016. THE ISSUES ARE THE SAME AND THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO THE PLAINTIFF IN THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION, THE LATTER BEING A COMMON REASON FOR DENIAL OF CONSOLIDATION. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO CASE # 619980, WHICH EFFECTIVELY WAS DISMISSED WHEN THE DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' VS. LYDIA KASHEVAROFF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 5, 2007, LINE 3, DEFENDANT LIDYA KASHEVAROFF'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT WITH MORE PARTICULARITY TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF BAKER V. BEACH AIRCRAFT CORP. 39 CAL. APP.3RD 315, 321, IN PARTICULAR THE 3RD ELEMENT. AS TO CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE BASIS FOR DEFENDANT HAVING A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP WITH PLAINTIFF OR PLEAD ACTUAL FRAUD BASED UPON CONCEALMENT WHERE A DUTY TO DISCLOSE EXISTS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KINRY LOUIE VS. THOMAS HARDMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 5, 2007, LINE 7 DEFENDANT THOMAS HARDMAN'S Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint IS OVERRULED. THE ALLEGATION THAT NOTICE WAS PROVIDED IS SUFFICIENT TO MEET PLEADING REQUIREMENTS. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH KELLY VS. ANNETTE SHELTON et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 5, 2007, LINE 8 DEFENDANT ANNETTE SHELTON, LARRY SHELTON, ALEXANDRA SHELTON-JIMMERSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED; REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. ASSUMING ANTI-SLAPP APPLIES TO UD CASES, PLAINTIFF'S ACTION IS BASED ON A LOGICAL FACTUAL BASIS AND NOT IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S PETITIONING ACTIVITIES. =(302/PJM/PB)
